🇺🇦🛢️🔥 𝗦𝘁𝗿𝗶𝗸𝗲 𝗼𝗻 𝗟𝘂𝗸𝗼𝗶𝗹-𝗣𝗲𝗿𝗺𝗻𝗲𝗳𝘁𝗲𝗼𝗿𝗴𝘀𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗲𝘇 | 𝗔𝗩𝗧-𝟰 𝗨𝗻𝗶𝘁 𝗗𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗿𝗼𝘆𝗲𝗱

BLUF: In the early morning of April 30, 2026, Ukrainian long-range drones, including at least one Lyutyi, struck the Lukoil-Permnefteorgsintez refinery in Perm, hitting the AVT-4 primary distillation unit, specifically the vacuum column, with fire spreading to the atmospheric rectification column. The resulting damage shut down one of Russia's largest refineries, processing approximately 13 million tons of crude per year.

The AVT-4 is the heart of primary oil processing which, under reduced pressure, converts heavy crude residue into vacuum gas oil, a critical feedstock for catalytic cracking and further refining. 

The atmospheric column separates crude into gasoline, kerosene, diesel, and fuel oil. 

With both columns damaged and offline, AVT-4 is effectively destroyed. Downstream units lose feedstock, forcing a major shutdown of the plant. 

This directly hits both civilian fuel supply in the Urals, Russian army logistics and operations, and oil revenues to pay the light bills at all of Putin's palaces.

Location: 57.910531, 56.122374

Sources: Open-source video | Ukrainian Military | Lukoil

#OSINT #RussianBloodOil #StandWithUkraine

Ukrainian forces struck the sanctioned Cameroonian-flagged VLCC MARQUISE (IMO: 9315745) approximately 120 NM SW of Tuapse. 

According to the AFU General Staff, the vessel was adrift and potentially waiting to enter port in Tuapse once the security situation improved or waiting for another tanker to perform an at-sea transfer.

The AFU did not specify the means of the strike but it is likely they used a USV (Magura or similar).

Coincidentally, a NATO (Italian) RQ-4D Phoenix ISR UAS, based out of Sigonella, was operating over the Black Sea in the general area of the MARQUISE.

#OSINT #RussianBloodOil

🇷🇺🫏🇺🇸🍊 𝗣𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗻 𝗖𝗮𝗹𝗹𝘀 𝗧𝗶𝗺𝗲𝗼𝘂𝘁... 𝗔𝗴𝗮𝗶𝗻

Around April 3, Zelensky proposed an Easter ceasefire, which Putin rejected. More than a week later, Putin declared one unilaterally as his own initiative, effective April 12 at 16:00 for 32 hours. Russian forces violated it 2,935 times before it expired.

On April 29, Putin called Trump, who praised Russia's Easter truce during the conversation. He made no mention of the fact that it was proposed by Zelenskyy. 

Putin then announced he was prepared to declare another ceasefire for May 9, Trump supported it, and Kremlin aide Ushakov announced it afterward.

Ukraine was not on the call. Zelensky directed his team to contact Washington to find out what had been agreed about their country without their knowledge.

The parade is already stripped down, with no tanks, no "no-analogue" wunderwaffen, no hypersonic tree nuts, and no boarding school cadet sons of the rich and famous. 

What Putin needed was a quiet sky over Moscow on May 9, and he got Trump to help provide it.

Ukraine started none of this and has no reason to stop fighting on Russia's parade schedule. It has every reason to remind Moscow what range looks like.

Sources: Ukrinform | Euronews | Reuters | BBC Russian Service

#OSINT #Ukraine

kept.text:
The human eye just saw a color that does not exist in nature.

Scientists at UC Berkeley did what was long considered impossible: they created “olo” — a brand-new hue that lies completely outside the natural human visual spectrum.

Using a technique called Oz, they fired precision lasers directly at individual medium-wavelength cone cells in the retina. By bypassing the usual overlapping signals our eyes have evolved over 300,000 years, they delivered a pure, never-before-seen signal straight to the brain.

The result is a hyper-saturated blue-green so vivid and alien that participants described it as breathtakingly beautiful — more intense than any turquoise, cyan, or peacock feather on Earth. Only a handful of people have ever witnessed it
.
Right now it only works in a tightly controlled lab. 

Subjects must lie perfectly still while lasers paint their retinas with microscopic micro-doses. The effect is temporary.

But the deeper revelation is profound.

Our vision evolved inside a narrow biological window. 

There is an entire hidden world of color — potentially millions of additional hues — sitting just beyond what nature ever allowed us to perceive.

For the first time, we’ve hacked our own hardware and peeked behind the curtain.

The universe didn’t change. Our perception of it just cracked open.

The light has always been there. We simply didn’t have the key — until now. 

[Fong et al. (2025). "Novel color via stimulation of individual photoreceptors at population scale." Science Advances, 11(16), eadu1052. DOI: 10.1126/sciadv.adu1052]

quoted_tweet.text:
Researchers have found a way to let the human brain see an entirely new color.

A team at the University of California, Berkeley, has achieved what was long considered impossible: the discovery of “olo,” a color that lies completely outside the natural human visual spectrum.

Using a revolutionary technique called Oz, scientists deployed high-precision lasers to target and stimulate thousands of specific medium-wavelength cone cells in the retina. By bypassing the usual overlap in how our eyes process light, they created a pure, unprecedented signal that the brain had never encountered before.

The result is a hyper-saturated blue-green hue that participants described as strangely alien yet breathtakingly beautiful.

For now, experiencing olo is only possible in a tightly controlled lab setting. Subjects must remain perfectly still while receiving carefully calibrated laser “microdoses” directly to their retinas. Though the effect is temporary, the breakthrough is profound.

Researchers believe this technology could one day help treat color blindness and potentially unlock the ability for humans to perceive millions of additional colors.

More importantly, it proves that our brains are far more capable of processing new sensory information than previously thought — revealing a hidden world of color that exists just beyond the limits of natural human vision.

[Fong et al. (2025). "Novel color via stimulation of individual photoreceptors at population scale." Science Advances, 11(16), eadu1052. DOI: 10.1126/sciadv.adu1052]
